data.body: Plan-review PASS (advisory=20, signed; 8 unanimous criticals). Triage: the criticals correctly flag that gap-labels 'G-5'/'G-10' would map to ids G5/G10 — but G5 ALREADY exists (decomposition), and is_overlay() only recognizes r'T\d+[a-e]?', so a G-prefixed id is NOT treated as an overlay. Resolution: register the two overlays under the next free Txx ids — T13 (prohibition-enumeration) and T14 (CI-trigger/release-infra) — so is_overlay() recognizes them, no collision, and route_criteria's overlay path applies. Prompts: plan_review_T13.md / plan_review_T14.md; tests keep the gap-label names (test_g5_prohibition/test_g10_citrigger) per the AC. Added T13/T14 to CANONICAL_LLM (the parity gate is bidirectional). Both get overlay_routing:llm + exec:AGENT (own context window = the dedicated overlay). Per T5b/T8: G-5 (T13) gets an explicit fail-open/abstain rule like G-10 (T14). ADR 0034 records the LLM-routed enumeration-overlay pattern + the G-label->Txx mapping. Claimed BEFORE the CANONICAL_LLM bump so the claim matches the attestation's registry_version.
